title: Which Iconic Meryl Streep Character Are You, Actually?
dek: One woman, eight legendary personas — take this quiz to find out whether your soul is more Miranda Priestly or more Donna Sheridan.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers — just go with your gut (or your inner glacial stare).

questions.question: How do you handle a coworker who turns in sloppy work?

answers.text: I don't yell. I just say "That's all" and let the silence do the damage.
I deliver a calm, devastating monologue about competence and legacy.
I show them how it's done, with a smile and way too much butter.
Eh, we'll figure it out over sangria. Life's too short.
A formal dinner where I'm the only one allowed to talk politics.
Cooking something absurdly ambitious just because I can.
Singing ABBA at the top of my lungs on a Greek terrace.
Sitting alone in a diner, watching my old life from across the street.

questions.question: Someone cancels plans on you last minute. Your reaction?

answers.text: No matter — I'll just make something delicious instead.
More wine for me and my girlfriends, then!
I take it as a sign the universe wants me somewhere else entirely.
I bury it quietly. I've survived worse than a cancelled plan.

questions.question: Pick a work style that sounds most like you:

answers.text: Whatever gets done, gets done — I'd rather be having fun.
I left my career to 'find myself,' then came back sharper than ever.
I do what's asked of me and keep my real feelings locked away.
I say the quiet part loud, whether anyone asked or not.

questions.question: How do you deal with a big secret from your past?

answers.text: I own it publicly, therapy included, no matter who judges me.
I carry it silently, because saying it out loud might break me.
I weaponize it at the dinner table when I need everyone to suffer with me.
I keep it locked in a box, only opened after I'm gone.

questions.question: A stranger asks you for directions. What happens next?

answers.text: I answer carefully — I don't trust strangers easily anymore.
I size them up and decide whether they deserve my time.
This is exactly how the most important four days of my life started.
I have an assistant for that.
Brutal honesty disguised as "just telling the truth."
A passion so intense I remember it for the rest of my life.
Being impressed is the closest thing I have to affection.
Loyalty to duty above all else — feelings are for later.

questions.question: How do your friends/family describe you behind your back?

answers.text: "Quiet. You'd never guess what she gave up."
"Terrifying, but somehow you still want her approval."
"Divisive. Iron-willed. Doesn't apologize, ever."
"Larger than life — and yes, she really is that tall."

questions.question: Someone challenges your authority in front of others. You:

answers.text: Destroy them with one whispered sentence and move on.
Stand my ground even as the whole room turns against me.
Laugh it off and offer them a plate of something amazing.
Deflect with a joke and a group hug.

questions.question: Pick the vacation that sounds most like you:

answers.text: A tightly scheduled state visit with zero downtime.
A cooking course in the French countryside I never want to leave.
A sun-soaked Greek island wedding that spirals delightfully out of control.
A solo trip to California to completely rebuild my identity.

results.name: Miranda Priestly
results.subtitle: The Devil Wears Prada (2006)
results.description: You lead with icy precision, not volume — power, to you, is never having to raise your voice. People call you cold; you call it standards. Everyone secretly wants to be you, and you already know it.

results.name: Margaret Thatcher
results.subtitle: The Iron Lady (2011)
results.description: You're divisive by design — steadfast, controversial, and completely unwilling to soften yourself for anyone's comfort. You'd rather be respected than liked, and you've made peace with the fact that not everyone gets to have both.

results.name: Julia Child
results.subtitle: Julie & Julia (2009)
results.description: You're larger than life in the best way — enthusiastic, warm, and completely unbothered by looking a little ridiculous if it means doing what you love with your whole chest. People remember rooms you walked into.

results.name: Donna Sheridan
results.subtitle: Mamma Mia! (2008)
results.description: You're chaotic-good energy in human form — messy history, three possible ex-lovers, and still somehow the most fun person at any party. Life doesn't have to make sense to be beautiful.

results.name: Joanna Kramer
results.subtitle: Kramer vs. Kramer (1979)
results.description: You value radical honesty over easy comfort, even when it makes you the villain of somebody else's story. You left to find yourself, and you're not sorry — you came back stronger, whatever anyone else thinks of the timeline.

results.name: Sophie Zawistowska
results.subtitle: Sophie's Choice (1982)
results.description: You carry things quietly, and it takes people a long time to realize how much you're actually holding. Your strength isn't loud — it's in the sheer fact that you're still standing at all.

results.name: Violet Weston
results.subtitle: August: Osage County (2013)
results.description: You say the quiet part loud, usually at the dinner table, usually to everyone's horror. You call it "truth-telling"; your family calls it something else entirely — but nobody can say you're not honest.

results.name: Francesca Johnson
results.subtitle: The Bridges of Madison County (1995)
results.description: You're steady and a little overlooked — until someone actually looks. Underneath the routine is a whole ocean of feeling you rarely let show, and when it finally does, it changes everything.

sources.title: Meryl Streep wins 3rd acting Oscar for 'Iron Lady' - Today
